ref: Korah, Gatam, and Amalek. These and Esau's other descendants lived in the land of Edom.
text: ni Kɔra ni Gatam ni Amalɛk. Bɛna n-daa nyɛ Ɛlifaz bidibisi bɛn nyɛ nanima Ɛdom tiŋgbɔŋ ni. Bɛ daa nyɛla Ada yaansi.
translated: This is Kora, Gatam, and Amalɛk. They were three brothers who lived in the town of Edom. They were also known as the Ada clan.

ref: years to buy your sheep and goats. During that time you kept changing my wages.
text: Yi mi ni n tum tuma ni n yaa zaa ti yi ba.
translated: I am going to the market to buy something for you.

ref: The Lord said to Abram, “I brought you here from Ur in Chaldea, and I gave you this land.”
text: Di saha ka o daa lan yɛli Ibram, “Mani n-nyɛ Yawɛ ŋun daa yih’ a Ur din be Kalidianim’ tiŋgbɔŋ ni na ni n ti zaŋ tiŋgbɔŋ ŋɔ ti a ka a su li.”
translated: He then said to Abraham, “I am not Yawɛ who went to the land of Kalidianim’ and returned to tell us about it.”

ref: The Lord said, “They now know the difference between right and wrong, just as we do. But they must not be allowed to eat fruit from the tree that lets them live forever.”
text: Ka Yawɛ ŋun nyɛ Naawuni ŋɔ daa yɛli, “Nyama, pumpɔŋɔ ninsal’ bemi kaman ti ni be shɛm, ka mi zaɣ’ suŋ ni zaɣ’ biɛɣu, ka di pa shɛli ka o ti pɔhigi nyɛvili tia la walli di m-be hal ni saha din ka bahigu.”
translated: When God created man, He said, “Look, I have given you all the plants and trees bearing fruit, and all the grains, and I have given you animals to eat, and everything that moves on the earth, and all the fish in the sea. They are all yours to use as food.”

ref: God said, “I command the earth to give life to all kinds of tame animals, wild animals, and reptiles.” And that's what happened.
text: Ka Naawuni daa lan yɛli, “Binnɛma balibu kam, bɛnya tiŋgbani zuɣu; niɣi mini binnɛma bɛn gbamdi tiŋa ni mɔɣu ni biŋkɔbiri balibu kam.” Ka di shiri niŋ lala.
translated: When the Creator finished speaking, He said, “Let the waters under the heavens be gathered together into one place, and let the dry land appear.” And it was so.
